trm:term.trm:name: Adoption
trm:term.trm:definition: The process of bootstrapping a project to use clayers. Adoption plants embedded XSD schemas into the project's .clayers/schemas/ directory, creates a starter specification with index and revision files, and amends the project's agent instructions file (CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md) with the spec-first development workflow. Adoption is idempotent: re-running on an already-adopted project checks freshness and reports which components are outdated, requiring the --update flag to overwrite them.

trm:term.trm:name: Onboarding skill
trm:term.trm:definition: A Claude Code skill file generated by the --skills flag during adoption. The skill provides structured, phase-by-phase guidance for an AI agent to systematically analyze an entire codebase, create comprehensive specifications with prose, terminology, organization, relations, and LLM descriptions, map all code via artifact traceability, and iterate on coverage, connectivity, and drift until the specification is complete. The skill template is embedded in the binary and written to .claude/skills/clayers-onboard/SKILL.md with project name substitution.

trm:term.trm:name: Review artifacts skill
trm:term.trm:definition: A Claude Code skill file generated alongside the onboarding skill by the --skills flag. This skill provides guidance for reviewing and improving existing artifact mappings, ensuring end-to-end coverage between specification and code in both directions. It covers drift resolution with root-cause analysis, exemption auditing, mapping quality improvement (favoring precise over broad mappings), and revision comparison. Written to .claude/skills/clayers-review-artifacts/SKILL.md with project name substitution.

pr:section.pr:title: The adopt command
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: Bootstrap a project with clayers schemas and development workflow.
pr:section.pr:p: The adopt command makes any project clayers-ready in a single invocation. It takes an optional path argument (defaulting to the current directory) and performs three operations:

pr:ol.pr:li: Plant schemas. All 12 XSD layer schemas, catalog.xml, and postprocess.xslt are embedded in the binary at build time via include_str!. The command writes them to .clayers/schemas/ in the target project.

Create starter spec. A skeleton clayers/<project>/ directory is created with an index.xml and revision.xml. The project name is derived from the target directory name. This step is skipped if a clayers/ directory already exists.

Amend agent file. The command looks for CLAUDE.md first, then AGENTS.md. If found, it appends the spec-first development workflow section. If neither exists, it creates AGENTS.md. Content is wrapped in HTML comment markers (<!-- clayers:adopt -->) for idempotent updates.

pr:section.pr:p: When the target project has already been adopted (detected by the presence of .clayers/schemas/ with XSD files or agent file markers), the command switches to freshness-checking mode. It compares each planted schema and the agent instructions against the embedded versions and reports what is outdated, missing, or current. Without the --update flag, it exits with an error after reporting. With --update, it overwrites outdated files and replaces the content between markers.
pr:section.pr:note: Schemas are embedded at build time, so clayers adopt is a zero-dependency bootstrap: no network access or external schema files are needed.

pr:section.pr:title: Spec-first development workflow
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: The workflow injected into agent files by clayers adopt.
pr:section.pr:p: The adopted workflow follows the principle of spec first, code second. Before writing any implementation code, developers and agents update the specification to describe what they are building. The workflow then proceeds through validation, implementation, artifact mapping, and quality iteration.

The injected workflow includes guidance to drive spec coverage to 100% as a natural way to discover what needs to be implemented. Every spec node should be mapped to implementing code via artifact mappings, and the coverage, connectivity, and drift tools provide continuous feedback on the mapping quality.

Plans are kept in the spec using the pln:plan layer, making them versioned, queryable, and linked to the concepts they implement. This replaces ad-hoc planning with structured, traceable plans that live alongside the specification.

pr:section.pr:title: Skill generation
pr:section.pr:shortdesc: The --skills flag generates Claude Code skills for codebase onboarding and artifact review.
pr:section.pr:p: The --skills flag on the adopt command generates Claude Code skill files in .claude/skills/ in the target project. All skill templates are embedded in the binary as an array via include_str!, with {{PROJECT_NAME}} placeholders replaced at write time. Skills participate in the freshness tracking system and can be refreshed via --update.

In interactive mode (when stdin is a TTY), the adopt command suggests generating skills by default. In non-interactive mode (piped input, CI), skill generation is skipped unless --skills is explicitly passed.

pr:ol.pr:li: clayers-onboard. Comprehensive codebase onboarding: discovery, terminology extraction, spec creation, artifact mapping, quality iteration, and final validation. Guides agents from an empty spec to 100% coverage.

clayers-review-artifacts. Artifact mapping review and improvement: bidirectional coverage analysis (spec-to-code and code-to-spec), drift resolution with root-cause analysis, exemption auditing, mapping quality improvement (favoring precise over broad), and revision comparison. Ensures end-to-end traceability is maintained as code evolves.

org:task.org:purpose: The CLI adopt command bootstraps a project with clayers schemas and spec-first workflow instructions.

org:concept.org:purpose: Describes the spec-first development workflow that gets injected into agent files during adoption.

org:task.org:purpose: The --skills flag generates a Claude Code skill that guides agents through comprehensive codebase onboarding.

rel:relation.rel:note: The adopt command implements the adoption concept.
The adopt command plants layer schemas, requiring understanding of the layer concept.
The starter spec creates nodes that participate in the ID space.
The workflow is the process guidance component of adoption.
Skill generation is an optional step within the adopt command flow.
The adopt-skills section describes how the onboarding skill concept is realized in the CLI.
The generated skills provide detailed, agent-executable guidance that refines the general spec-first workflow into concrete phases with success criteria.
The review-artifacts skill assumes the onboarding skill has been used first (or artifact mappings already exist) and focuses on maintaining and improving existing coverage.

art:mapping.art:note: The adopt module implements all adopt command logic: fresh adopt, freshness checking, update flow, schema planting, starter spec creation, and agent file amending.
The embedded module provides build-time schema embedding via include_str!, making adopt a zero-dependency bootstrap.
The adopt module is the canonical implementation of the adoption concept.
The WORKFLOW_CONTENT constant contains the spec-first development workflow that gets injected into agent files.
WORKFLOW_CONTENT constant: the spec-first development workflow text embedded in the binary for injection into agent files.
is_adopted: detects whether a project has already been adopted by checking for .clayers/schemas/ with XSD files or agent file markers.
check_freshness: compares each planted schema and agent instructions against embedded versions, reports outdated/missing/current.
find_agent_file, extract_between_markers helpers, FreshnessStatus enum, and FreshnessItem struct.
should_generate_skills, plant_skill, and check_skill_freshness: skill generation logic including TTY detection, file creation, and freshness checking.
plant_schemas: writes all embedded XSD schemas, catalog.xml, and postprocess.xslt to .clayers/schemas/.
create_starter_spec: creates clayers/<project>/ skeleton with index.xml and revision.xml.
amend_agent_file and update_adopted: agent file injection with HTML comment markers, skill file handling, and idempotent update flow.
adopt() public entry point: dispatches between fresh adopt and update-check, with optional skill generation.
Basic adoption tests: fresh adopt creates schemas/spec/agents, prefers CLAUDE.md over AGENTS.md, detects adoption state.
Update, freshness, and skill tests: update flow, schema verification, skill generation, freshness checking, and project name substitution.
Adopt command variant in Command enum (with --skills flag), dispatch in run(), and cmd_adopt function.
AGENT_GUIDANCE_XML constant: embeds agent-guidance.xml via include_str! for runtime extraction of workflow and skills.
The agent guidance spec file: contains workflow instructions, onboarding skill, and review-artifacts skill as llm:node elements with CDATA sections. Content is extracted at adopt time.

spec:clayers.llm:node: The adopt command bootstraps any project to use clayers. It embeds all 12 XSD layer schemas plus catalog.xml and postprocess.xslt at build time (via include_str!), then writes them to .clayers/schemas/. It creates a starter clayers/<project>/ directory with skeleton index.xml and revision.xml (skipped if clayers/ exists). It amends the agent file (CLAUDE.md preferred, then AGENTS.md, or creates AGENTS.md) with the spec-first development workflow, wrapped in HTML comment markers for idempotent updates. Re-running on an already-adopted project checks freshness and requires --update to overwrite outdated components.

Adoption is the bootstrap process for making a project clayers-ready. It plants schemas, creates a starter spec, and injects workflow instructions. The process is idempotent: subsequent runs detect adoption via .clayers/schemas/ or agent file markers, compare against embedded versions, and report freshness. The --update flag overwrites outdated components. Schemas are embedded at build time for zero-dependency bootstrap.

The spec-first development workflow injected by clayers adopt establishes: (1) update spec before coding, (2) validate, (3) implement, (4) map spec to code with artifacts, (5) iterate on coverage/connectivity/drift quality. Plans go in the spec using pln:plan. The driving metric is 100% spec coverage: every spec node mapped to implementing code.

An onboarding skill is a Claude Code skill file (.claude/skills/ clayers-onboard/SKILL.md) that guides AI agents through complete codebase specification. It is generated by clayers adopt --skills, embedded in the binary with {{PROJECT_NAME}} placeholders, and participates in the freshness tracking system. The skill covers six phases: codebase discovery, terminology extraction, spec file creation, artifact mapping, quality iteration (coverage/connectivity/ drift), and final validation. It includes XML templates, clayers commands, and success criteria for each phase.

A review-artifacts skill is a Claude Code skill file (.claude/skills/ clayers-review-artifacts/SKILL.md) generated alongside the onboarding skill. It guides agents through reviewing and improving existing artifact mappings for end-to-end coverage. Covers eight phases: spec-to-code coverage (unmapped nodes), code-to-spec coverage (uncovered ranges), drift resolution with root-cause analysis, exemption auditing, mapping quality review (preferring precise over broad), connectivity cross-check, optional revision comparison, and final verification. Includes a decision framework for map-vs-exempt choices with guidance to ask the user when uncertain.

The --skills flag on clayers adopt generates Claude Code skills for codebase work. Two skills are embedded: clayers-onboard (initial onboarding from empty spec to 100% coverage) and clayers-review- artifacts (maintaining and improving coverage as code evolves). In interactive mode (TTY), prompts to generate by default. In non-interactive mode, --skills must be explicit. Templates are embedded as a SKILLS array via include_str!, written to .claude/ skills/{name}/SKILL.md with project name substitution. Skills participate in freshness tracking and can be added to already- adopted projects without requiring --update.
